Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove multiresource budgets, owned workers, origin fairness, rejection isolation, and drain; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Drain Handles, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Feed Ts Drain Handles record says origin-aware bounded async ingestion pool looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Feed Ts Drain Handles.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Ts Drain Handles for origin-aware bounded async ingestion pool.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop claims, abort fetches, await settlements, expire or finalize leases, clear timers and listeners, close the pool, and assert no unexpected handles.
